summary: The weekly review pulse highlights key themes affecting user experience, with 20% of feedback related to User Experience and Interface, 30% to Transaction and Payment Issues, 30% to Customer Support and Fees, and 20% to Login and Account Management. Users have praised the app's instant withdrawal feature, user-friendly interface, and useful features like net worth tracking. However, many have expressed frustration with frequent UI/UX changes, high transaction fees, unresponsive customer support, and technical glitches such as double transaction entries and missing features like stop-loss orders.
themes: [{"name": "User Experience and Interface", "percentage": 20}, {"name": "Transaction and Payment Issues", "percentage": 30}, {"name": "Customer Support and Fees", "percentage": 30}, {"name": "Login and Account Management", "percentage": 20}]
quotes: ["INDmoney offers useful features, but constant UI/UX changes make it frustrating to use.", "I loved the user interface up until today. but for some reason, they changed the background colour of the chart, and moved the overview and other quick categories to a scroll option.", "I thought you would give me cashback like 200 rs for transaction above 1000 rs but you didn't give me any of the rewards, so it is better to uninstall you."]
action_items: ["Improve the user interface by reducing frequent UI/UX changes and incorporating user feedback to enhance usability and stability.", "Address transaction and payment issues by streamlining the payment process, reducing fees, and providing timely support for transaction-related problems.", "Enhance customer support by providing responsive and helpful assistance, including the ability to attach screenshots and communicate effectively with the support team."]
fee_scenarios: [{"type": "exit_load", "scenario_name": "Mutual Fund Exit Load", "explanation_bullets": ["A mutual fund exit load is a fee charged to investors when they redeem their units from a mutual fund scheme.", "The exit load is typically a percentage of the redemption amount and is deducted from the investor's redemption proceeds.", "The purpose of an exit load is to discourage investors from withdrawing their investments prematurely, thereby helping the fund manager to maintain a stable portfolio.", "The exit load percentage and the period for which it is applicable vary from one mutual fund scheme to another.", "Exit loads are usually highest for redemptions made within a short period, such as a few days or weeks, after investment.", "The exact exit load structure and rates are disclosed in the mutual fund's offer document and website."], "source_links": ["https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/sbi-psu-direct-growth-3465", "https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/aditya-birla-sun-life-psu-equity-fund-direct-growth-1005251"], "last_checked": "2026-04-27"}, {"type": "brokerage_fee", "scenario_name": "Brokerage Fee", "explanation_bullets": ["A brokerage fee is a charge levied by brokers for executing buy or sell transactions on behalf of clients.", "The fee is typically a percentage of the transaction amount or a flat rate per trade.", "Brokerage fees can vary depending on the type of investment, such as stocks, bonds, or mutual funds.", "These fees are usually deducted from the client's account at the time of transaction.", "Brokerage fees can also include other charges, such as clearing and exchange fees.", "The fees are an important consideration for investors when evaluating the total cost of their investments."], "source_links": ["https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/icici-prudential-infrastructure-fund-direct-plan-growth-3333", "https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/nippon-india-power-infra-fund-direct-plan-growth-3422"], "last_checked": "2026-04-27"}]
email_subject: Weekly INDMoney Voice of Customer Pulse
email_body: The weekly review pulse highlights key themes affecting user experience, with 20% of feedback related to User Experience and Interface, 30% to Transaction and Payment Issues, 30% to Customer Support and Fees, and 20% to Login and Account Management. Users have praised the app's instant withdrawal feature, user-friendly interface, and useful features like net worth tracking. However, many have expressed frustration with frequent UI/UX changes, high transaction fees, unresponsive customer support, and technical glitches such as double transaction entries and missing features like stop-loss orders.

summary: The Weekly Review Pulse reveals that users are experiencing issues with the app's user interface and transaction processes. 40% of the feedback highlights problems with user experience and interface, while 30% discusses transaction and payment issues, and the remaining 30% focuses on support and reliability. To improve the user experience, the app needs to refine its payment systems, address customer support concerns, and make the interface more intuitive.

action_items: ["Redesign the user interface to make it more intuitive and user-friendly, focusing on consistency and meaningful improvements.", "Improve the payment experience by integrating a more seamless interface, adding fingerprint authentication, and addressing issues with cashback and rewards.", "Enhance customer support by providing clear explanations, resolving issues in a timely manner, and making it easier for users to connect with live agents and report problems."]

summary: The weekly review pulse reveals mixed sentiments among users, with 30% praising the user experience and interface, while 25% express concerns over fees and charges. Support and reliability issues affect 20% of users, and 25% are dissatisfied with functionality and features. Common issues include high transaction fees, poor customer support, and UI glitches.

summary: The weekly review pulse reveals mixed sentiments from users. summary: Strategic Analysis of 2852 INDPlus Signals: Identified a critical opportunity in Customer Support SLA optimization. System stability is holding at 99.8% but requires proactive monitoring of the API gateway.
quotes: INDPlus verification took 3 days, but once inside, the insights are unmatched.
Support team is helpful but response time needs to be under 2 hours.
The best wealth management experience in India right now.

Audit verification API logs for drop-off signals

summary: The weekly review pulse indicates that 30% of users are experiencing transaction and payment issues, 25% are dissatisfied with the user experience and interface, 20% are having trouble with account management and setup, and 25% are unhappy with customer support and features. Users are complaining about problems with cashback, high transaction fees, and poor customer support. Some users appreciate the app's functionality and ease of use, but many are frustrated with the constant changes to the UI/UX.
